items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement projects and spending
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine Mountain View's planned capital projects and infrastructure spending for fiscal year 2026-27. This is when residents can learn what physical improvements and investments the city is prioritizing.

items.headline: City Council to Consider Changes to Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing an ordinance that would amend its Residential Tenant Protections Program. The changes could affect rental housing rules and tenant rights in the city.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan Gets Status Update
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on a plan to improve San José library facilities and customer experience. This could affect local library services and physical improvements residents use.

items.headline: Downtown historic building seeks bonus floor space
items.summary: The owner of 405 Kipling Street is requesting to upgrade the building's historic status from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is proposing to rezone and redevelop two properties on San Antonio Road into an 8-story building with 167 apartments (28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant new housing and a zoning change for the area.

items.headline: Planning hearing for 333 Franklin Street project postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. The project at 333 Franklin Street will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed, affecting whatever development is planned at that location.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully listed in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what infrastructure or development this contract covers.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward with New Consultant
items.summary: The council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project. This infrastructure project could affect traffic patterns and safety at a major South Bay intersection.
